heroHeadline: Hegseth Refuses Article 5 Reaffirmation; Trump Calls NATO 'Paper Tiger' and Mulls US Exit
heroSubtitle: Defense Secretary Hegseth's March 31 refusal to reaffirm Article 5 — 'that's the president's decision' — followed Trump calling NATO a 'paper tiger' and declaring the US is 'strongly considering' leaving the alliance. Spain closed its airspace to US jets, Italy blocked Sigonella, and Poland rejected Patriot redeployment in a coordinated pushback. Secretary Rubio pledged a full NATO reassessment after the Iran war concludes.
footerNote: Data sourced from NATO HQ, White House, US Department of Defense, Reuters, Bloomberg, The Telegraph, TIME, Politico, German Federal Foreign Office, US News, Asharq Al-Awsat, GV Wire, Al Jazeera, Financial Times, Stars and Stripes
